task.json and README.md explicitly state the occupation.

pi_items.metadata_value: Cross-modality CT vs MR imaging correlation and comparison
pi_items.verified_value: Cross-modality CT vs MR imaging correlation and comparison

pi_items.source: verifier.py and export_result.sh strictly check only for 2 PNG exports (>=15KB), >=3 numerical measurements, and specific regex matches in the text report; visual annotations, dual viewports, window/level adjustments, and precise percentage calculations are not enforced.

privileged_info_summary: The task utilizes real clinical DICOM datasets downloaded from rubomedical.com. While the task instructions and metadata describe a clinical scenario requiring the comparison of a CT scan and an MRI scan (crossmod_ct and crossmod_mr), analyzing the downloaded files (dicom_viewer_0002.zip and dicom_viewer_0003.zip) reveals that both datasets are actually multi-frame X-Ray Angiography (Modality: XA) sequences (with 96 and 17 frames respectively), not CT or MR scans. Furthermore, the evaluation logic in verifier.py and export_result.sh does not check for dual viewports, window/level adjustments, visual annotations, or accurate percentage calculations; it only verifies the creation of two PNG files (minimum 15KB each) and a text report containing specific CT/MR keywords, at least three numerical measurements, and comparative language.
